content.full_text:
May 11,11977

Lawrence H. Kay
Construction Industry Force Account Council
301 Capitol Mall, Suite 402
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Kay:

Thank you for your letter of May 2, 1977 requesting advice
with respect to the Construction Industry Force Account
Council's potential filing responsibilities under the
Political Reform Act.

Section 86108 of the Political Reform Act provides that the
following persons shall file the statements required by
section 86109 (Form 650):

"(a) Any person who employs or contracts for the
services of one or more lobbyists, whether
independently or jointly with other persons;
and

(b) Any person who directly or indirectly makes
payments to influence legislative or administrative action of two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value in any month, unless
all of the payments are of the type described
in Section 82045(c)."

Since the Council does not employ or contract for the services
of a lobbyist, as stated in your letter, it does not have a
filing responsibility with respect to Section 86108(a). However, if the Council makes payments to influence legislative
or administrative action, as defined in Section 82045, in
excess of $250 in any month, a filing responsibility with
respect to Section 86109(b) is created, unless all of the payments are of the type which benefit officials or members

of their immediate families. It should be noted that the time
spent by employees who devote less than 10% of their time to
these activities does not have to be included. (2 Cal Adm.
Code Section 18621) I am enclosing Form 650 and the instruction manual which fully describes the filing responsibilities
of persons under Section 86108(b).

If I can be of further assistance, do not hesitate to contact
me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Alan Herndon
Compliance Representative

AH:bw

Enclosure

ASSOCIATED GENERAL CONTRACTORS OF CALIFORNIA

San Diego

San Diego, California 92103
(714) 293-2900
(714) 293-2934

Ventura
1530 E. 17th Street - Suite 5
Santa Ana, California 92701
(714) 547-487

SKILL [SEAL] INTEGRITY

May 2, 1977

Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Construction Industry Force Account Council

Gentlemen:

The above referenced organization is a non-profit
corporation established by Contractor Trade Associations
and Building Trade Unions throughout the state to counter
the ever increasing utilization of force account construction by cities, counties, and state agencies. One
of the major purposes of CIFAC is to develop a uniform
legislative program which is being developed by a committee representing members of the organization.

In developing the legislation      CIFAC is concerned with
the regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission
with respect to lobbying activities. All of the members
of the CIFAC Legislative Committee are involved in legislative activities and have in their employ registered
lobbyists who are reporting monthly as required under the
regulations of FPPC. Furthermore, each one of the lobbyists employers is filing its required FPPC form 650 reports.
It is our opinion that CIFAC should not be required to file
any lobbyist employer reports since we do not have any
registered lobbyists in our employ and are utilizing our
members lobbyists who are currently registered and reporting
to the FPPC. However, we would appreciate receiving an
opinion with respect to the above referenced subject.
Should you desire any further information in respect to
this matter feel free to call upon me.

OFFICERS
J. Stanley Harris, President
Bert J. Mark, Sr. Vice President
Robert S. Love, Jr., Secretary
A.J. Dian, Treasurer
Richard B. Munn, Executive Director

REPLY TO: Sacramento

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Lawrence H. Kay
Counsel

LHK/mea

sections.question: Should the Construction Industry Force Account Council (CIFAC) be required to file lobbyist employer reports under the Political Reform Act, given that CIFAC does not employ registered lobbyists but instead utilizes members' registered lobbyists?
sections.conclusion: Since the council does not employ or contract for the services of a lobbyist, it does not have a filing responsibility with respect to Section 85108(a). However, if the council makes payments to influence legislative or administrative action in excess of $250 in any month, a filing responsibility with respect to Section 85108(b) is created, unless all payments are of the type which benefit officials or members of their immediate families. Time spent by employees who devote less than 10% of their time to these activities does not have to be included.
sections.question_synthetic: The Construction Industry Force Account Council seeks clarification on whether it must file Form 650 lobbyist employer reports under Section 85108 of the Political Reform Act, given that it does not directly employ lobbyists but coordinates legislative activities through member organizations that have registered lobbyists.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that CIFAC has no filing obligation under Section 85108(a) because it does not employ or contract for lobbyists. However, CIFAC must file under Section 85108(b) if it makes payments to influence legislative or administrative action exceeding $250 per month, with certain exemptions for payments benefiting officials or their families and for de minimis employee time under 10%.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (May 2, 1977 from Lawrence H. Kay) and FPPC response letter (May 11, 1977 from Alan Herndon). Text quality is poor with OCR errors but key statutory language and conclusions are identifiable. Question and conclusion sections are synthesized from the letter body as no formal section headers exist.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should the Construction Industry Force Account Council (CIFAC) be required to file lobbyist employer reports under the Political Reform Act, given that CIFAC does not employ registered lobbyists but instead utilizes members' registered lobbyists?

CONCLUSION: Since the council does not employ or contract for the services of a lobbyist, it does not have a filing responsibility with respect to Section 85108(a). However, if the council makes payments to influence legislative or administrative action in excess of $250 in any month, a filing responsibility with respect to Section 85108(b) is created, unless all payments are of the type which benefit officials or members of their immediate families. Time spent by employees who devote less than 10% of their time to these activities does not have to be included.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Construction Industry Force Account Council's inquiry regarding lobbying disclosure filing requirements. FPPC concludes the council need not file as a lobbyist employer under Section 85108(a) but may be required to file under Section 85108(b) if it makes qualifying payments to influence legislative action.
